> *来源：FXStreet · 2026/3/4*



Gold hovered close to **$5,200 per ounce on Thursday**, building on gains for a second straight session as ongoing Middle East tensions continued to drive risk‑off flows into safe‑haven assets. Traders have bid up **XAU/USD** amid fresh hostilities between U.S., Israeli and Iranian forces, while the U.S. dollar’s recent pause supported bullion’s advance. The conflict’s persistence has underpinned haven demand and capped broader market risk appetite. 

Market participants noted the metal’s resilience despite bouts of volatility, with some positioning defensively ahead of key data later this week. Analysts point out that geopolitical uncertainty remains a dominant driver for gold, keeping prices around the **$5,200** area assion on Thursday as traders seek safety amid the ongoing war in the...


Market participants noted the metal’s resilience despite bouts of volatility, with some positioning defensively ahead of key data later this week. Analysts point out that geopolitical uncertainty remains a dominant driver for gold, keeping prices around the **$5,200** area as investors weigh intensifying conflict against broader macro cues.
